category_title: Students with SEBD (ADHD/ADD) who struggle with passive learning and traditional instruction
minimal_context: The student is sitting alone in a mainstream mathematics class, tasked with copying trigonometry formulas from the whiteboard.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please sit down at your desk and copy the trigonometry formulas from the whiteboard.

STUDENT: I feel bored all the time sitting down. Why do we have to copy all the time from the whiteboard and gaze at it?

TUTOR: Just focus on the board and write down what you see.

STUDENT: Mentally I cannot understand this. I can't just sit here and be like a statue.

TUTOR: You need to work alone and finish copying the notes.

STUDENT: I prefer standing up and working in groups rather than alone! (Stands up abruptly and starts fighting with the student at the next desk)

options.A: Respond to the student by saying, 'Don't say you can't, you can.'
options.B: Respond to the student by saying, 'I'm so proud of you! Good job, keep going.'
options.C: Provide visuals, such as videos, computer programs, and pictures, for the trigonometry formulas.
options.D: Withhold attention for the fighting, and provide lots of attention when the student raises their hand.
